mnemonics: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away (for the layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
keyPoints: TCP/IP is a conceptual model and set of communications protocols used in the Internet and similar computer networks.
It is often described as a four-layer model (Link, Internet, Transport, Application) rather than the OSI model's seven layers.
The core protocols are TCP for connection-oriented communication and IP for addressing and routing packets.
commonMistakes: Confusing the TCP/IP model's four layers with the OSI model's seven layers.
Assuming TCP and IP are the same thing; IP handles addressing and routing, while TCP handles reliable data delivery.
Thinking the TCP/IP protocol suite is only comprised of TCP and IP; it includes many other protocols like UDP, HTTP, FTP, etc.
